_backup_metadata.title: PCP Managing Excellence Journey Wave 5 – Feedback Survey.

details.title: PCP Managing Excellence Journey Wave 5 – Feedback Survey.

pages.description:
Thank you for your participation and engagement throughout our PCP Managing Excellence Journey Wave 5.
We invite you to evaluate your experience and give us your feedback.
We value your opinion, which will help us improve.

headings.heading:
This program has helped me to contribute better to the objective of.......
Consider the score from 1-5: 1 = strongly disagree; 5 = strongly agree.

rows.text: a. Growing together as one team in the PCP growth and leadership.
c. Improving how I interact with others.

headings.heading:
The program has inspired and supported me........
Consider the score from 1-5: 1=strongly disagree to 5=strongly agree

rows.text: a. In my personal development as a leader.
b. In my personal growth and well-being.
a. demonstrates robust knowledge of the workshop content.
b. creates an environment of trust in which we can address important matters for our team.
c. generates and facilitates discussions that help understand the proposed methodology.

headings.heading: What are the 3 most positive aspects of the Workshop Sessions for you?
What do you think would be appropriate to modify to improve its effectiveness?
Is there anything we can help you with to develop your personal and team leadership?
What do you think you need as an organization to develop?

answers.text: well organized; best moderator Rolf;helpful contents
prolong another 3 months of the Journey, it ended too quick) my feeling)
sure, topics about Conversation, mindset, vullnerable, are important and helpful topics
continue learning and utilize the learned knowledge; refreshthe last Journeys' participants; set up PG group among differnt waves of MEJ
to set up PG group among new-former MEJ participants, to help refresh the 'old' participants, broaden contact

1.Establish a workshop environment of mutual trust, and "We are never alone". 
3.Inspire and support personal development, growth and wellbeing, focus on "I" and "WE" level to influence "IT".
3.Cultivate our mindsets (learner, player and growth), self-driven.
Add successful case sharing for theoretical knowledge from industry insiders or MEJ seniors.
Drive "Mentor-Mentee" program approach.

1.Clear mission and goals, shared values
2.Transparent and efficient collaboration system
3.A complete and transparent talent reserve and development mechanism
In any case, one is the primary person responsible for one's own growth and career development.

- emotions vs. leading
- giving a positive feedback
- connections across PCP
I think 3 sessions will be OK.
At the moment no.

Opennes and be more focused.
On operational level is to much no aded value activities connected with BSH organisation.
Thank you for good organisation.
trust, vulnerability and support
not per se the MEJ itself but rather after a few months / years  following up on lived values by former participants
commitment to openness and trust in each other as well as willingness to change esp. processes
Thank you for this very valueable journey together!

1. work with bigger and smaller groups during session
2. external facilitator
3. long time of programme (not finished in one week, but take a few months)

1. I'd mix participation groups, workshops perform within people from different locations and roles but peer groups create with the closest environment. Then we can meet new people and learn from each other but in case real change we can already do it in close around.
No, in the place where I am now, I have everything to grow, thank you.

Re-consider if people on leaders positions are true leaders, unfortunately I don't see it sometimes. There are specific skills requested, organization can evaluate and should evaluate it. Positive leadership is power, not only on high level roles, but starting with team leaders as well. They have big impact on people, with wrong mindset there is more harm than good.

I believe it is worth to spread this programm to all employees, maybe not with support of external company due to costs, but in PCP we have community of internal trainers/coaches which I think are able to pass the content and inspire people. Then the whole organization can speak one language and keep mindset alive.
openess, we had a small peer grup of 4 (really good)! regularyty of the meetings to keep the learnings fresh in mind
more trainings like this, respectively continous exchanges like in the peer groups
thanks to Chris!
openness, exchange, support each other
regular refreshment sessions + bad practice sessions
meet and discuss things with BSH colleagues outside of the normal way-of-working

as some colleagues had problems to join the meetings perhaps it would help to plan 2 or 3 cpl. days... I know that this is problematic for the homework between the workshops, but I am sure this could be somehow compensated in the big group of participants
not for the moment

not only to introduce and offer things like this MEJ project, but also live it in the daily operative working mode... my feeling is, that many colleagues (from mgmt.) who were part of MEJ already, forgot most fo the things he learned in this workshops
